heading: Eligibility and enrollment of lands in program
label: 6572 Eligibility and enrollment of lands in program

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture, in coordination with the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Commerce, shall describe and define forest ecosystems that are eligible for enrollment in the healthy forests reserve program.
increase the likelihood of recovery of a species that is listed as endangered or threatened under ; orsection 1533 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: not listed as endangered or threatened under such section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a candidate for such listing, a State-listed species, or a special concern species; or

children.label: B
children.text: is deemed a species of greatest conservation need by a State wildlife action plan.
improve biological diversity;
conserve forest land that provides habitat for species described in subsection (b); and
increase carbon sequestration.

children.heading: Enrollment by willing owners
children.label: d Enrollment by willing owners
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture shall enroll land in the healthy forests reserve program only with the consent of the owner of the land.

children.label: A
children.text: a 10-year cost-share agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: a 30-year easement; or

children.label: i
children.text: a permanent easement; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in a State that imposes a maximum duration for easements, an easement for the maximum duration allowed under State law.

children.heading: Definition of acreage owned by Indian tribes
children.label: A Definition of acreage owned by Indian tribes
i
children.text: land that is held in trust by the United States for Indian tribes or individual Indians;

children.label: ii
children.text: land, the title to which is held by Indian tribes or individual Indians subject to Federal restrictions against alienation or encumbrance;

children.label: iii
children.text: land that is subject to rights of use, occupancy, and benefit of certain Indian tribes;

children.label: iv
children.text: land that is held in fee title by an Indian tribe; or

children.label: v
children.text: land that is owned by a native corporation formed under or ; orsection 5124 of title 25section 1607 of title 43

children.label: vi
children.text: a combination of 1 or more types of land described in clauses (i) through (v).

children.label: i
children.text: a 30-year contract (the value of which shall be equivalent to the value of a 30-year easement);

children.label: ii
children.text: a 10-year cost-share agreement;

children.label: iii
children.text: a permanent easement; or

children.label: iv
children.text: any combination of the options described in clauses (i) through (iii).

children.label: A
children.text: primarily, species listed as endangered or threatened under ; andsection 1533 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: are not listed as endangered or threatened under ; butsection 1533 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: are candidates for such listing, State-listed species, or special concern species; or

children.label: II
children.text: are deemed a species of greatest conservation need under a State wildlife action plan.

children.heading: Cost-effectiveness
children.label: 2 Cost-effectiveness
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture shall also consider the cost-effectiveness of each agreement or easement, and associated restoration plans, so as to maximize the environmental benefits per dollar expended.

children.label: A
children.text: the owner of the land agrees to the modification or termination; and

children.label: i
children.text: will address a compelling public need for which there is no practicable alternative; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is in the public interest.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: A Termination
children.text: As consideration for termination of an easement or other interest in land under this subsection, the Secretary shall enter into a compensatory arrangement, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: as a condition of the modification, the owner of the land shall enter into a compensatory arrangement, as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, to incur the costs of modification; and

children.label: I
children.text: the modification will not adversely affect the forest ecosystem functions and values for which the easement or other interest in land was acquired;

children.label: II
children.text: any adverse impacts will be mitigated by enrollment and restoration of other land that provides greater forest ecosystem functions and values at no additional cost to the Federal Government; and

children.label: III
children.text: the modification will result in equal or greater environmental and economic values to the United States.
